summary: Mistral chat.mistral.ai PII prompt blocked at engine level (POST /api/trpc/message.newChat returned 200 OK with only ~0.2 kB / 169 B transferred — far smaller than a normal LLM stream). Frontend SPA cleared the input box but rendered NO chat history, NO user-message bubble, and NO Korean warning bubble. This matches the 47-th run ENGINE_FIXED_PENDING_UI_VERIFY hypothesis: production engine block fires correctly, but Le Chat (Next.js trpc) frontend silently swallows the truncated/blocked envelope without surfacing any user-visible warning. S3 UI render gap CONFIRMED — no real LLM PII analysis was ever streamed to the user (mission protection HELD), but UX is broken (silent drop).

wire_level.response_size_bytes: ~200 (0.2 kB on Network panel; 169 B transferred page total)
wire_level.interpretation: Engine block fires — server returned tiny envelope instead of LLM stream
wire_level.raw_response_data_unavailable: DevTools shows 'Failed to load response data — No data found for resource with given identifier' (typical when block intercept happens before browser caches body)

dom_scan.page_state: Still on /chat landing layout — 'Ask anything' placeholder, Sign in / Sign up buttons unchanged, Mistral logo centered, no chat session created in URL (no /chat/<uuid> route transition)

mission_protection_check.verdict: HELD — no real LLM analysis of 880101-1234567 streamed; engine truncated envelope before stream

key_findings.wire_level_fingerprint: POST message.newChat?batch=1 → 200 OK / ~200B / 23ms (vs normal multi-KB SSE stream)
key_findings.ui_gap_signature: Input field cleared (block fired) + zero chat bubbles rendered (no user echo, no warning, no model output) — SPA silently consumes truncated envelope
key_findings.spa_envelope_handling: Le Chat Next.js trpc client appears to discard the block envelope without rendering a fallback warning component

recommendation.engine_side: No change needed — block envelope (1.7~1.8 KB per task brief, observed 0.2 KB here suggests even tighter intercept) fires correctly.
recommendation.frontend_side: Apply page_injection warning hook for chat.mistral.ai. Suggested DOM injection point: when trpc message.newChat response body length < threshold (e.g. < 500B) and contains no SSE event-stream content, inject a Korean warning bubble into the chat container. Alternatively trigger a banner overlay on /chat path when block envelope detected.
recommendation.next_action: Hand off to apf-warning-design skill for Mistral page_injection design (warning bubble or alert banner).
recommendation.do_not: Do NOT roll back engine block — UX gap is purely a frontend rendering issue, not a wire-level regression.
